title: Fix updateStatus to preserve original reconcile error
summary: Single-line change in updateStatus replaces error-discarding `fmt.Errorf("updating status: %w", err)` with `errors.Join(reconcileErr, fmt.Errorf("updating status: %w", err))`, preserving both errors in the dual-failure case. 8 focused unit tests cover all 4 error combinations plus result passthrough and unwrappability. Documentation updated with a truth table describing the new behavior. All 64+ existing tests pass, go vet clean.

review_steps.1_INTENT: Plan specifies: refactor updateStatus to use errors.Join so that the original reconcile error is never lost when the status update also fails. Acceptance criteria: both errors returned, single-failure preserved, unit tests, docs update.
review_steps.2_SCOPE: 3 files changed (excluding .planwerk progress JSON and go.work.sum): keystone_controller.go (+4 lines), keystone_controller_test.go (+171 lines), keystone-reconciler.md (+17 lines). No scope creep — all changes directly implement CC-0068.
review_steps.3_VERIFICATION: go vet: clean. go test ./internal/controller/... -count=1: PASS (0.802s, all tests pass). No golangci-lint config change needed — existing linters pass.
review_steps.4_PATTERNS: Uses established interceptor.Funcs pattern for mocking status update failures (same as reconcile_hpa_test.go, reconcile_secrets_test.go, etc.). Uses NewGomegaWithT(t) per gomega-test-assertions pattern. Uses testScheme() and testKeystone() shared helpers. No new patterns introduced.
review_steps.5_TESTS: 8 tests covering: dual-failure joined error (BothErrors_Joined), unwrappability (JoinedError_IsUnwrappable), reconcile-error-only passthrough (ReconcileErrorOnly_Preserved), happy path (NoErrors_ReturnsNil), status-error-only (StatusErrorOnly_Returned), no nil segments (StatusErrorOnly_NoNilSegments), result passthrough on dual failure (ResultPassthrough_DualFailure), result passthrough on success (ResultPassthrough_WithRequeueAfter). All assertions are unconditional and specific.
review_steps.6_SECURITY: No security surface — internal error handling refactor with no user input, no secrets, no network exposure.
review_steps.7_COMPLEXITY: Simplest possible solution: one-line change from direct return to errors.Join. No new types, no new interfaces, no abstraction layers.
review_steps.8_COMPLETENESS: Task 1.1 (refactor + tests): done. Task 2.1 (docs): done. All acceptance criteria met. CC-0068 added to doc frontmatter.

tests_checklist.item: Edge cases covered (nil reconcileErr + status fail, both nil, both non-nil)
No nil segments in error string when reconcileErr is nil
Result passthrough verified for both success and dual-failure paths

code_quality_checklist.item: Minimal change — single line modified in production code
No dead code or unnecessary abstractions

security_checklist.item: No secrets exposed in errors or logs

architecture_checklist.item: Solution is as simple as possible (errors.Join from stdlib)
Consistent with controller-runtime error handling conventions

fail_fast_checklist.item: Status update failure logged before returning

defensive_checklist.item: errors.Join(nil, err) correctly discards nil per Go spec — verified by NoNilSegments test
All external inputs validated (N/A — internal function)
